terms.definition: Python's built-in mutable mapping of unique keys to values, backed by a hash table.
The generic name for a key→value collection that uses hashing for O(1) average operations; Python's dict is a hash map.
An immutable, hashable label under which a value is stored in a dictionary.
An object that has a stable hash value (`__hash__`) and consistent equality (`__eq__`); required for use as a dict key or set element.
A position in the hash table's internal array where one or more colliding entries are stored.
A dict subclass that calls a factory function to supply a default value for missing keys.
A dict subclass specialized for counting hashable items and ranking by frequency.
